parentQuestion: Which receptor type is found at ALL autonomic ganglia?

scaffolds.q: Nicotinic receptors are ligand-gated ion channels that open when ACh binds.
scaffolds.explain: Nicotinic receptors are ionotropic — ACh binding directly opens an ion channel allowing Na⁺ influx and K⁺ efflux, rapidly depolarizing the cell. Trace this pathway:: this makes ganglionic transmission fast. The receptor IS the channel. Beautiful directness.

scaffolds.q: There are two main types of cholinergic receptors: nicotinic and muscarinic.
scaffolds.explain: Chart this course: into cholinergic pharmacology. Nicotinic receptors (named for nicotine) are ion channels. Muscarinic receptors (named for muscarine) are G-protein coupled. ACh activates both, but the effects are very different. Location determines which receptor matters.
